text.title: Performance Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Eight most common performance anti-patterns — N+1 queries, sync external calls, missing caching, over-fetching, no connection pooling, large payloads, missing indexes, and premature optimization — each with detection signal and concrete fix.

rule.statement: Profile before optimizing. Agents will refactor cold paths because they look messy; always demand an APM trace or flame graph identifying the hot path before accepting a performance change proposal.
rule.rationale: Cold-path refactoring wastes engineering cycles and introduces risk with no latency benefit. Hot paths are identified by sampling, not by reading code.

list.item: N+1 Queries — detection: ORM query log shows one query per loop iteration (1 list query + N detail queries). Fix: eager loading (JOIN, prefetch_related in Django, includes in ActiveRecord, DataLoader in GraphQL). Typical improvement: 100 items = 101 queries → 2 queries.

Sync External Calls — detection: APM traces show thread blocked on outbound HTTP/gRPC with no async context. Fix: async/await, connection pools, circuit breakers with timeouts. Return 202 Accepted and process async for non-blocking user flow.
No Caching — detection: cache hit ratio < 60% on hot paths, or cache layer absent entirely. Fix: add multi-layer caching per caching-architecture methodology. See content/01-patterns.xml.

Over-Fetching — detection: response payload contains columns never read by the caller; API returns full objects when only IDs are needed. Fix: field selection (GraphQL), pagination (cursor or offset), API versioning to add selective endpoints.

No Connection Pooling — detection: DB connection count grows with request concurrency, TLS handshake time visible in traces, "too many connections" errors under load. Fix: PgBouncer (PostgreSQL), ProxySQL (MySQL), SQLAlchemy pool, HikariCP (Java). Size per formula: core_count × 2.

Large Payloads — detection: network transfer time dominates trace waterfall; payload > 100KB in a typical API response. Fix: pagination (default 20–100 items), Brotli/gzip compression, binary serialization (Protobuf, MessagePack) for internal APIs.

Missing Indexes — detection: EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows Seq Scan on large tables; pg_stat_user_tables.seq_scan rising. Fix: index columns in WHERE and JOIN ON clauses, index foreign keys, add partial/covering indexes for hot queries.

Premature Optimization — detection: team spends time micro-benchmarking cold code, introducing complexity without a measured performance problem. Fix: measure first (APM baseline + load test), optimize only paths appearing in the 80th percentile of trace spans.
Step 1 — APM dashboards: identify slow endpoints (Datadog, Grafana Tempo, New Relic, Honeycomb). Filter to p95 and p99, not mean.
Step 2 — Distributed traces: find the slow span within the slowest endpoint. Attribute to one layer (DB, external call, CPU).
Step 3 — Flame graphs: for CPU-attributed spans, use py-spy (Python), async-profiler (JVM), or pprof (Go) to identify the hot function.
Step 4 — Database profiling: EXPLAIN ANALYZE (PostgreSQL), slow query log (MySQL), pg_stat_statements for aggregate patterns.
Step 5 — Network analysis: if network contributes >20% of trace, check compression, payload size, and CDN cache hit ratio.
Agents conflate throughput and latency — a change that improves throughput (batch processing) often increases individual p95 latency. Require both metrics per change proposal.
LLM optimization suggestions frequently change algorithmic complexity without measurement; require Big-O before/after and a load test proving the improvement.
"Add an index" suggestions ignore write amplification: each additional index adds overhead to every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. Require a write-cost estimate alongside the read benefit.
Auto-scaling recommendations skip cold-start latency cost; demand pod startup time in the proposal and pre-scaling strategy for known traffic spikes.
Cache hit ratio, DB connection pool saturation, GC pause frequency, and TLS handshake counts are the four metrics agents miss most often — name them explicitly in the prompt.
Human-in-loop gates: SLO sign-off, capacity plan review, post-deploy SLO review at 24h and 7d.
